90: **Canada**, level on 4 points with one other team from their group (position not yet separated), face **Morocco**, level on 7 points with one other team from their group (position not yet separated), at 10:00 AM PT at NRG Stadium in Houston — Canada's first-ever Round of 16 match. Canada beat South Africa 1-0 in the Round of 32 thanks to Stephen Eustaquio's injury-time volley, but arrive without Ismael Kone (broken leg, vs Qatar) and with Alphonso Davies — who has not started a minute this tournament following a hamstring recurrence — potentially making his first start since March 2025. Morocco eliminated the Netherlands on penalties in the Round of 32, with Issa Diop's 91st-minute equaliser forcing extra time before a 3-2 shootout victory; Ismael Saibari, whose move to Bayern Munich was confirmed the day after that win, scored three goals in the group stage and leads their attack, though centre-back Chadi Riad (substituted in the 75th minute vs the Netherlands) is questionable. The winner advances to the quarter-finals in Boston to face **France** or **Paraguay**.
89: **Paraguay**, level on 4 points with one other team from their group (position not yet separated), face **France**, 1st in **Group I** on 9 points, at 2:00 PM PT at Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia. Paraguay beat Germany 4-3 on penalties after a 1-1 draw in the Round of 32 — Julio Enciso scoring the goal, Orlando Gill the shootout hero — but arrive with centre-back Omar Alderete out (knee, sustained vs the group-stage finale), while Enciso and Gabriel Avalos are both carrying knocks and being monitored; Diego Gomez returns from suspension in midfield. France beat Sweden 3-0 in the Round of 32 to extend a run in which they have scored 3+ goals in each of their four matches at this tournament — 13 total — with Mbappe on six goals (level with Messi in the Golden Boot race) and Olise providing five assists, the most by any player at a single tournament since 1994; **Paraguay** have failed to beat **France** in five previous attempts (W0 D2 L3), losing 1-0 in extra time in the 1998 last-16. The winner faces the **Canada**–**Morocco** winner in the quarter-finals in Boston.
88: **Australia**, level on 4 points with one other team from their group (position not yet separated), face **Egypt**, level on 5 points with one other team from their group (position not yet separated), at 11:00 AM PT at AT&T Stadium in Dallas — Egypt's first-ever knockout match at the finals. Egypt's injury crisis is severe: captain Mohamed Salah is a major doubt (hamstring strain, subbed off at 57 minutes vs Iran), Ahmed Fattouh is effectively ruled out with a hamstring tear, and Mohamed Abdelmonem carries ankle bruising; if Salah cannot start, Omar Marmoush of Manchester City is expected to lead the attack. Australia have never won a knockout match at the finals — losing to Italy in 2006 and Argentina in 2022 — and arrive with Mathew Leckie and Jacob Italiano already ruled out of the tournament with hamstring and adductor injuries respectively; coach Tony Popovic reports no further injury concerns. The winner advances to the Round of 16 in Atlanta to face **Argentina** or **Cabo Verde**.
86: **Argentina**, 1st in **Group J** on 9 points after winning all three group games and scoring eight goals, meet **Cabo Verde**, 2nd in **Group H** on 3 points, at 3:00 PM PT at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami — the Blue Sharks' first-ever match at the finals in the knockout rounds and their tournament debut as a nation. Cabo Verde midfielder Telmo Arcanjo is out with a hamstring injury, while left-back Sidny Lopes Cabral returns after serving a one-match suspension against Saudi Arabia; goalkeeper Vozinha, 40, who kept clean sheets against **Spain** and **Saudi Arabia** across the group stage, faces Lionel Messi — on six tournament goals and in seven consecutive matches at the finals — alongside Lautaro Martinez and Julian Alvarez, who has recovered from an ankle issue that limited him early in the tournament. Cristian Romero is also in line to return from a minor knee complaint for Argentina. The winner faces **Australia** or **Egypt** in the Round of 16 in Atlanta on July 7.
87: **Colombia**, 1st in **Group K** on 7 points, face **Ghana**, 3rd in **Group L** on 4 points, at 6:30 PM PT at Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City — the first-ever meeting between the two nations at the finals. Ghana are without talisman Mohammed Kudus (pre-tournament injury) and Mohammed Salisu, Antoine Semenyo (ankle knock, vs Croatia) is a game-time decision, goalkeeper Lawrence Ati-Zigi is a fitness doubt with Benjamin Asare expected to start, and Thomas Partey missed Ghana's win over Panama after being denied entry to Canada due to ongoing legal proceedings in the UK — though he is available here; Colombia's Daniel Muñoz leads their scoring charts with two goals from right-back, and James Rodriguez has six goals and four assists across 11 career finals matches since 2014. The winner faces either **Switzerland** or **Algeria** in the Round of 16.
